name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework 1.0
description: Voluntary framework providing structured guidance for AI risk management. Function-based rather than tier-based.
verification_note: NIST AI RMF 1.0 remains the current published, voluntary baseline; the Govern/Map/Measure/Manage function structure this dataset models is unchanged. A revision is in progress -- as of 2026-04, NIST had released a concept note for an AI RMF Profile on Trustworthy AI in Critical Infrastructure, and per America's AI Action Plan is revising the framework to remove references to misinformation, DEI, and climate change. No document labeled 'AI RMF 2.0' has been published. NEEDS_OWNER_REVIEW: watch for the revision's completion, since category wording (not the four-function structure) may change. Verified via web search 2026-07-09. Sources: NIST AI Risk Management Framework (https://www.nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework); NIST AI RMF Development (https://www.nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework/ai-rmf-development); ISPartners, 'NIST AI RMF 2025-2026 Updates' (https://www.ispartnersllc.com/blog/nist-ai-rmf-2025-2026-updates-what-you-need-to-know-about-the-latest-framework-changes/).

govern.name: GOVERN
govern.description: A culture of risk management is cultivated and present. Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization to enable and improve AI risk management.
categories: GV-1: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices
GV-4: Organizational teams document AI risks
GV-5: Engagement with relevant AI actors
GV-6: Address AI risks from third party software and data
elevated_attention_triggers: AI system in production without documented risk policy
Use of third-party LLMs or vendor AI without due diligence
AI initiative spanning multiple business units without clear accountability
critical_attention_triggers: AI system deployed in regulated industry without formal risk committee oversight
AI vendor relationship without contractual risk allocation
AI initiative crossing jurisdictional boundaries without legal review

map.name: MAP
map.description: Context is recognized and risks related to context are identified. Establishes the foundation for risk identification.
categories: MP-1: Context is established and understood
MP-2: Categorization of the AI system is performed
MP-3: AI capabilities, targeted usage, goals, and expected benefits documented
MP-4: Risks and benefits are mapped for all components
MP-5: Impacts to individuals, groups, communities, and society characterized
elevated_attention_triggers: AI affects consumers or vulnerable populations
Use of biometric or sensitive data
critical_attention_triggers: Consequential decisions affecting natural persons (credit, hiring, healthcare)
Foundation model use without provenance documentation
Use cases involving children or other protected populations

measure.name: MEASURE
measure.description: Identified risks are assessed, analyzed, and tracked. Encompasses both quantitative and qualitative metrics.
categories: MS-1: Appropriate methods and metrics are identified and applied
MS-2: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics
MS-3: Mechanisms for tracking identified AI risks over time
MS-4: Feedback about efficacy of measurement is gathered
elevated_attention_triggers: Single-metric evaluation (accuracy only) without fairness or robustness assessment
critical_attention_triggers: High-stakes AI without quantitative fairness assessment
Generative AI without hallucination rate measurement
AI system with measured performance significantly below claimed performance

manage.name: MANAGE
manage.description: Risks are prioritized and acted upon based on a projected impact. Allocation of resources and ongoing risk treatment.
categories: MG-1: AI risks based on assessments are prioritized, responded to, managed
MG-2: Strategies to maximize AI benefits and minimize negative impacts
MG-3: AI risks and benefits from third-party entities managed
MG-4: Risk treatments documented and monitored regularly
elevated_attention_triggers: No incident response runbook for AI failures
No defined ownership for ongoing AI risk management
critical_attention_triggers: High-risk AI deployed without HITL or rollback mechanism
No incident management process for AI-driven decisions
Risks identified during MAP not addressed in MANAGE
